top_stories.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
top_stories.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on stolen authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: The article examines how AI agents are emerging as highly privileged identities that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are not designed to handle. It argues that standing privilege models are inadequate for AI agent workloads, pointing to the need for just-in-time access and dynamic authorization approaches.

top_stories.title: UNC6783 Hackers Use Fake Okta Pages in Corporate Breach Campaign
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is conducting corporate breach campaigns by impersonating support staff and using fake Okta login pages to steal credentials and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems. The campaign combines social engineering with phishing infrastructure mimicking Okta's authentication pages to capture credentials and exfiltrate sensitive data.

top_stories.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs accounting for more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws, making this a significant update for PAM and access management security.

top_stories.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
top_stories.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to adopt in response. It focuses on how breaches increasingly exploit legitimate credentials, making them appear as normal activity and challenging traditional security approaches.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: Russia's Fancy Bear APT group continues conducting global cyber espionage campaigns targeting organizations of varying technical sophistication. Security experts recommend patching and zero trust implementation as non-negotiable defensive measures against the threat group.

news.title: Product showcase: Ente Auth encrypts, backs up, and syncs 2FA
news.summary: Ente Auth is a free, open-source authenticator app that generates and stores one-time passcodes for two-factor authentication with cross-device sync and backup capabilities. The app supports QR code and manual account setup, and allows importing from other authenticator apps.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials in over $20 million in fraud attempts. The W3LL toolkit was a commercial phishing-as-a-service platform specifically designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to Windows users in Chrome 146, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft. DBSC binds browser sessions to a specific device using cryptographic keys, making stolen session cookies unusable on other devices.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" bypasses traditional detection by proxying real login pages and relaying credentials including MFA tokens between victims and legitimate sites in real time. The service effectively defeats MFA protections by acting as a man-in-the-middle, capturing usernames, passwords, and one-time codes as they are entered.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring MFA capabilities to web-based applications. No additional details about the product's specific features, target market, or technical implementation are provided in the available description.

news.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
news.summary: Specops published a piece outlining five ways Zero Trust architecture strengthens identity security, focusing on credential theft and privilege escalation risks. The article covers identity-first Zero Trust principles including access limitation, device trust enforcement, and lateral movement prevention.

news.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity capabilities as part of a broader strategy to dominate AI infrastructure. The acquisition targets non-human identity and machine identity management, positioning Cisco's security portfolio to govern agentic AI systems.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is a new open-source tool written in Rust (Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the host via PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to provide cryptographic proof of token possession, differentiating it from other OIDC-based SSH authentication approaches.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge. The interview covers his experience at IBM and CyberArk and addresses how organizations can bring visibility and governance to non-human identities.

news.title: Identity Protection in the AI Era
news.summary: The article presents a security framework focused on identity-first approaches incorporating zero trust enforcement, threat detection, and AI protection into a unified model. Key elements include visibility, threat response, and identity protection strategies relevant to modern IAM practices.

news.title: Google Chrome Update Disrupts Infostealer Cookie Theft
news.summary: Google has added Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) to Chrome 146, using hardware-based cryptographic keys to bind session cookies to specific devices on Windows. This directly counters infostealer malware that steals session cookies to bypass authentication, a common technique used to circumvent MFA protections.

news.title: To counter cookie theft, Chrome ships device-bound session credentials
news.summary: Google is shipping device-bound session credentials in Chrome to counter cookie theft attacks where infostealer malware extracts and exfiltrates authentication cookies. The feature cryptographically binds session credentials to the specific device, preventing stolen cookies from being used on attacker-controlled systems even when the cookies have extended lifetimes.

news.title: Google Chrome 146 Adds Device Bound Session Credentials to Stop Session Cookie Theft on Windows
news.summary: Google has introduced Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) in Chrome 146 for Windows, a security feature designed to prevent session cookie theft. DBSC cryptographically binds session credentials to a specific device, making stolen session cookies unusable on other machines.
